title: Rams Leapfrog Seahawks in 2026 NFL Offseason Power Rankings
body:
The NFL offseason has been a whirlwind of activity, with all 32 teams making significant moves to bolster their rosters ahead of the 2026 season. As the dust settles, a clearer picture is emerging of which teams are poised for success and which are facing an uphill battle. One of the biggest movers in the offseason power rankings is the Los Angeles Rams, who have jumped ahead of their NFC West rivals, the Seattle Seahawks. This surge can be attributed to a key addition that may not take the field, but is expected to have a profound impact on the team's performance: new offensive coordinator Mike McDaniel.

The hiring of McDaniel is a significant coup for the Rams, who are looking to rebound from a disappointing season. McDaniel's expertise is expected to breathe new life into the team's offense, which has been stagnant in recent years. His ability to design and implement innovative game plans will be crucial in unlocking the full potential of the Rams' talented roster. With McDaniel at the helm, the team is expected to become more dynamic and explosive, capable of competing with the top offenses in the league. This is a significant development, not just for the Rams, but for the entire NFC West, which is shaping up to be one of the most competitive divisions in football.

The Rams' offseason moves have been shrewd and calculated, addressing key areas of need and strengthening their roster. The addition of McDaniel is the icing on the cake, providing the team with a crucial edge in terms of strategy and game planning. The Seahawks, on the other hand, have been relatively quiet in the offseason, which may have contributed to their slide down the power rankings. While they still boast a talented roster, they will need to find a way to counter the Rams' newfound offensive prowess if they hope to keep pace in the division. The NFC West is shaping up to be a battle royale, with the Rams, Seahawks, and San Francisco 49ers all vying for supremacy.

The NFL offseason is a time of hope and optimism, with every team believing they have a chance to make a deep playoff run. However, as the power rankings indicate, some teams are better positioned than others. The Rams' aggressive approach to the offseason has paid dividends, and they are now firmly in the conversation as a top-tier team. The Seahawks, meanwhile, will need to regroup and refocus if they hope to reclaim their spot atop the division. The 2026 season is shaping up to be a thrilling ride, with plenty of twists and turns along the way.

As the NFL preseason approaches, teams will begin to take shape and the true contenders will emerge. The Rams and Seahawks will be two teams to watch closely, as they engage in a high-stakes battle for NFC West supremacy. The addition of Mike McDaniel to the Rams' coaching staff has been a game-changer, and it will be fascinating to see how he impacts the team's performance on the field. With the stage set for a thrilling season, football fans are in for a treat. The question on everyone's mind is: can the Rams sustain their momentum and make a deep playoff run, or will the Seahawks find a way to reclaim their spot atop the division?

As the 2026 NFL season kicks off, one thing is certain: the Rams are a team on the rise. With their newfound offensive firepower and a revamped coaching staff, they are poised to make some noise in the playoffs. The Seahawks, meanwhile, will need to find a way to counter the Rams' surge and reassert their dominance in the NFC West. The stage is set for a thrilling season, full of drama, excitement, and unexpected twists. As the season unfolds, it will be fascinating to see how these two teams, and the rest of the NFL, navigate the ups and downs of the 2026 campaign. With the offseason power rankings providing a snapshot of where each team stands, the real action is about to begin, and football fans couldn't be more excited.
